extra.message: User input from request.args.get('ip') is passed directly to subprocess.check_output() without any sanitization. An attacker can inject arbitrary arguments to the ping command (e.g., additional -c flags, or exploit interaction with the subprocess).
Hardcoded username ('admin') and password ('password123') are stored in plaintext in the source code. Anyone with read access to the codebase can gain administrative access to the application.

pickle.loads() is called on raw untrusted user input from request.data. pickle deserialization can execute arbitrary code, leading to remote code execution. An attacker can send a malicious pickle payload to achieve full server compromise.

User input from request.args.get('id') is concatenated directly into a SQL query string via string interpolation. An attacker can inject arbitrary SQL, e.g., ' OR '1'='1' -- , to bypass authentication or extract/modify database contents.

Flask application is run with debug=True, which enables the Werkzeug debugger. In production this exposes interactive stack traces, source code, and allows arbitrary code execution via the debugger PIN. This also leaks sensitive information in error responses.

Unsanitized subprocess output (which contains attacker-controlled input via the 'ip' parameter) is returned directly as an HTTP response with default Content-Type text/html. An attacker can inject HTML/JavaScript that will be rendered in the victim's browser.

The constructed SQL query containing unsanitized user input from request.args.get('id') is reflected back in the HTTP response with default Content-Type text/html. An attacker can inject HTML/JavaScript payloads via the 'id' parameter.

The string representation of a deserialized object from untrusted input is returned directly as an HTTP response. Since the attacker controls the pickle payload, they can craft an object whose __str__ returns malicious HTML/JavaScript.

The raw content from an external HTTP response (requests.get) is returned directly to the client without sanitization. If the upstream server is compromised or the response contains malicious content, it will be rendered in the user's browser.
